prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property  : Conformational rotation barrier about the F–C–C–F dihedral of 1,2-difluoroethane (gauche ↔ anti)
Level   : GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) in xtb
Geometry  : Relaxed dihedral scan (`conformational-analysis` skill). Starting coordinates taken from the supplied 12-difluoroethane.xyz; each of 24 points was re-optimized with the F–C–C–F dihedral constrained.
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge/Mult: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software  : xtb backend via chemkit conformational-analysis wrapper (ASE optimizer under the hood)
Convergence: all 24/24 scan points converged; integrity.checks report trustworthy = true; no warnings were returned.
Live log   : The calculation streamed to the live .out file noted at launch (tail-able during the run; path preserved alongside the `live.json` result file in the same temp directory).

Result
• Single dihedral scanned: F(1)–C(2)–C(3)–F(4)
  ‑ Minimum (anti) at 180 ° E = −9 901.5107 kcal mol⁻¹
  ‑ Maximum (eclipsed) at   0 ° E = −9 894.8510 kcal mol⁻¹
  ‑ Rotation barrier (interpolated) = 6.6604527 kcal mol⁻¹

No other rotatable bonds were detected for this molecule at the chosen granularity, so only one barrier is reported.

This value is a semi-empirical gas-phase estimate appropriate for rapid conformer ranking; absolute barriers from GFN2-xTB typically carry ≈1–2 kcal mol⁻¹ methodological uncertainty, but the relative trend (anti lower than eclipsed) is robust at this level.
